headline: US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury kills Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei and collapses Strait of Hormuz traffic by 70%, while Pakistan declares open war on Afghanistan and China sanctions 50 Japanese companies, producing the most volatile week for global markets since February 2022.

top_stories.title: Operation Epic Fury Decapitates Iranian Regime; Strait of Hormuz Collapses
top_stories.summary: On February 28, the US and Israel launched joint Operation Epic Fury, striking over 1,000 targets across 24 Iranian provinces including IRGC facilities, air defenses, missile sites, and nuclear infrastructure. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed when his official residence was destroyed. Iran retaliated with strikes on 27 US bases and Gulf Arab states, including missile hits on Dubai and Abu Dhabi. The Strait of Hormuz experienced a 70% traffic collapse after IRGC broadcast closure warnings and attacked three commercial vessels. The IRGC is consolidating control during a 40-day mourning period with no clear succession mechanism.
top_stories.investor_impact: Oil surged 13% with Brent at $72.48 and forecasts reaching $100/bbl if Hormuz remains disrupted. Gold spiked past $5,300/oz on safe-haven flows. Gulf state sovereign bonds face repricing. Energy-import-dependent economies (JPN at 10% energy independence, TWN at 5%, KOR at 15%) face inflation and growth headwinds. US recession probability jumped from 25-30% to 40-50%. Defense contractors (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman) are direct beneficiaries.

top_stories.title: Pakistan Declares Open War on Afghanistan; Nuclear-Armed State in Active Conflict
top_stories.summary: Pakistan declared open war on Afghanistan on February 27, launching Operation Ghazab Lil Haq with airstrikes targeting TTP and ISIS-K positions in Kabul and Kandahar. Pakistan rejected all calls for dialogue from Iran, the EU, Qatar, and China. Cross-border trade has been suspended and refugee flows are disrupting border provinces. Twenty people were killed in domestic protests over the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, compounding domestic instability. Political stability at -1.93 is among the worst in the dataset.
top_stories.investor_impact: Pakistan sovereign bonds (CCC+/Caa1) face further spread widening with political risk premium at 1,650 bps. PKR under renewed depreciation pressure. Defense spending surge will strain $18.4B FX reserves and complicate IMF program compliance. China's CPEC investments face elevated operational risk. Cross-border trade suspension disrupts Central-South Asian supply chains.

top_stories.title: SCOTUS Strikes Down IEEPA Tariffs; US Trade Policy in Constitutional Crisis
top_stories.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 on February 20 that the President lacks tariff authority under IEEPA, striking down the existing tariff framework. Trump pivoted to Section 122 of the Trade Act, imposing a 15% global tariff for 150 days, with additional tariffs of 20% cumulative on China and 25% on Canada and Mexico effective March 4. Companies are suing for over $134 billion in tariff refunds. The effective US tariff rate dropped from 16% to 9.1%, a structural shift in trade openness.
top_stories.investor_impact: Trade-exposed manufacturers face acute uncertainty. Canada (75%+ of exports to US) and Mexico (USMCA renegotiation 2026) are most vulnerable. The $134B+ refund question creates contingent fiscal liability. Importers and retailers benefit from lower effective tariffs. Section 122 authority is temporary (150 days), creating a policy cliff in mid-2026.

top_stories.title: China Sanctions 50 Japanese Companies and Restricts Rare Earth Exports
top_stories.summary: China sanctioned 50 Japanese companies on February 25, restricted rare earth exports, and imposed dual-use goods controls in response to PM Takaichi's Taiwan defense comments. This marks the worst Japan-China bilateral moment in decades and represents a new front in the global trade fragmentation. Japan's manufacturing sector faces supply chain disruption, while Japan accelerated defense spending to $58B, reaching its 2% of GDP target two years ahead of schedule.
top_stories.investor_impact: Japanese manufacturers with China rare earth dependencies face acute supply chain risk. Mitsubishi Heavy and Kawasaki Heavy benefit from defense buildup. Global rare earth supply chains face repricing. China-Japan decoupling accelerates supply chain diversification to ASEAN. Semiconductor and EV supply chains are particularly exposed to rare earth restrictions.

top_stories.title: Israel Faces Multi-Front War as Hezbollah Breaks Ceasefire and IRGC Retaliates
top_stories.summary: Israel's security situation critically deteriorated this week. Hezbollah broke the November 2024 ceasefire on March 1, firing rockets at northern Israel. IRGC launched retaliatory strikes including a missile that hit a road in West Jerusalem. Israel now faces a three-front war: Gaza, Lebanon, and Iran. Military spending reached 8.8% of GDP, the second highest globally. A March 31 budget deadline could trigger government dissolution. Moody's downgraded Israel to Baa1.
top_stories.investor_impact: Israeli sovereign bonds face repricing with multi-front conflict. Shekel under depreciation pressure. Technology sector FDI at risk from prolonged conflict and political uncertainty. Budget crisis creates additional fiscal risk. Military spending at 8.8% of GDP crowds out civilian investment.

regional_summaries.North America: The US faces converging crises: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs prompting a pivot to Section 122 (15% global tariff), while Operation Epic Fury against Iran drove recession probability to 40-50%. Inflation trending up to 2.7% from oil shock, debt at 125% of GDP on a growth trajectory as tariff revenue drops $175B. Canada and Mexico face 25% tariffs effective March 4. Canada's deep US trade integration (75%+ of exports) makes it uniquely vulnerable, with bilateral relationship health at strong_decrease. Mexico GDP trend is growth (1% to 1.5%) but judicial reform with only 13% voter turnout undermines rule of law.
regional_summaries.Europe: Germany launched a historic EUR 500B infrastructure fund and record EUR 88.5B defense spending while facing energy import pressure from the oil surge, with GDP at 0.2% trending to 0.9%. The UK underwent a major cabinet reshuffle after Deputy PM Rayner's resignation. Denmark called snap elections for March 24 over the Trump-Greenland standoff. ECB held at 2%; oil-driven inflation complicates the rate path. EU-Mercosur entered provisional application. Ukraine's counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km and Flamingo missiles struck Russian ICBM infrastructure; IMF approved $8.1B in new lending. Poland defense spending reached $38B. Georgia political crisis entered its second year with EU accession suspended until 2028.
regional_summaries.East Asia: China faces GDP deceleration (4.8% to 4.2%) amid US tariff escalation and sanctioned 50 Japanese companies with rare earth export controls -- the worst China-Japan bilateral moment in decades. NPC opens March 5. Japan's LDP supermajority enables constitutional revision with defense budget at $58B, reaching 2% of GDP target two years early. South Korea's Yoon was sentenced to life for insurrection; Lee government stabilizing with semiconductor recovery supporting economy. Taiwan faces acute vulnerability with 5% energy independence, 69% chokepoint exposure, and PLA blockade simulation. North Korea tested hypersonic missiles.
regional_summaries.South Asia: Pakistan's declaration of open war on Afghanistan dominates the region, with 20 killed in domestic protests over Iran strikes compounding instability. Sovereign credit at CCC+/Caa1 with political stability at -1.93. India faces significant oil shock exposure (imports 85% of oil via Hormuz-dependent routes), with inflation trending from 2.8% to 4% and rupee at 91.42/USD, but remains the fastest growing major economy at 6.2%. Bangladesh held its first post-uprising election with BNP winning a landslide (209 seats). Sri Lanka remains in selective default with 100.8% debt-to-GDP but GDP has recovered to 5%.
regional_summaries.Middle East & North Africa: The region faces its most severe crisis since the 2003 Iraq War. Iran's regime is in existential crisis following Khamenei's killing, with 42.4% inflation approaching hyperinflation. The Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse critically affects UAE (76% chokepoint exposure, missiles hit Dubai and Abu Dhabi), Qatar (66%, Al Udeid base struck), Kuwait (64%, Ali Al Salem base struck), Oman (62%), Saudi Arabia (52%), and Iraq (51%). Israel upgraded to critical alert with Hezbollah ceasefire collapse and IRGC direct retaliation creating a three-front war. Turkey positioned as potential mediator with 34.9% inflation. Egypt's Suez Canal revenues threatened by resumed Houthi attacks.
regional_summaries.Sub-Saharan Africa: Ethiopia has the highest risk premium globally at 2,700 bps, with selective default credit and Tigray conflict resumption threatening the 2022 peace agreement; elections scheduled June 1. Nigeria suffered a Boko Haram massacre killing 170+ amid 23% inflation and labor protests. Ghana and Sri Lanka remain in selective default territory. DR Congo's conflict affects cobalt and coltan supply chains. Sudan's civil war continues as one of the world's worst humanitarian crises. South Africa's JSE benefited from improved investor sentiment.
regional_summaries.Latin America: Argentina delivered strong legislative progress: Senate passed Milei's labor reform 42-28 and ratified EU-Mercosur 69-3, with monthly inflation decelerating to 2.9%. Brazil benefits from EU-Mercosur as the largest Mercosur economy, with Bovespa gaining 16.3% in February and BRL appreciating 2.1%. Mexico faces 25% US tariffs effective March 4 with judicial reform undermining investor confidence. Peru installed its 9th president in a decade with elections scheduled April 12. Colombia elections on March 8. Venezuela remains in turmoil after US capture of Maduro in January.
regional_summaries.Central Asia: Kazakhstan maintains stable 6.1% GDP growth, though oil export revenues face indirect Hormuz disruption risk. Uzbekistan continues steady growth. The broader region faces indirect effects from the Pakistan-Afghanistan war, with potential refugee spillovers and trade disruption along traditional routes. Russia's economic stress (21% central bank rate, 9% inflation, GDP at 0.6%) ripples through remittance-dependent economies in the region.
regional_summaries.Southeast Asia: Singapore's extreme trade dependence (current account 17.5% of GDP) creates vulnerability to the Hormuz shipping disruption; GDP trending down to 1.8%. Indonesia signed a $33B trade deal with the US, positioning itself in the US orbit amid China competition. Vietnam holds legislative elections March 15 with 70% chokepoint exposure. Philippines faces governance crisis as Marcos corruption scandal forced House Speaker and Senate President to step down. Thailand's PM Anutin formed a new coalition government on February 13.
regional_summaries.Oceania: Australia is a relative safe haven with Aaa/AAA credit rating and commodity export benefits from higher oil and mineral prices. RBA hiked rates to 3.85% on higher-than-expected inflation. ASX 200 posted its strongest month since 2019. New Zealand remains stable with minimal direct exposure to the Middle East conflict. Both economies face indirect effects through Asian trade linkages and oil import costs.

market_context.summary: Markets experienced a historic risk-off rotation driven by Operation Epic Fury and the Hormuz crisis. Gold surged past $5,300/oz intraday on safe-haven demand, oil spiked 13% on supply disruption fears with Brent at $72.48 and forecasts of $100/bbl if sustained, and US Treasuries rallied with 10Y yields falling below 4% for the first time in four months. The S&P 500 posted its largest monthly decline in a year while Brazil's Bovespa surged 16.3% monthly on EU-Mercosur optimism.

notable_moves.driver: Safe-haven demand from US-Iran military conflict; surged past $5,300 intraday
Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse; forecasts of $100/bbl if sustained
Flight-to-quality; fell below 4% for first time in four months; strongest monthly decline in a year
Safe-haven spillover from gold; correcting from all-time high of $121.67 in January
Supply disruption fears from Middle East conflict; advanced approximately 45 cents in February
Iran conflict, recession fears (40-50% probability), tariff uncertainty; Dow futures fell 517 points
EU-Mercosur optimism; BRL appreciation to strongest since May 2024
Controlled devaluation by CBRT; record low for lira despite 47.5% policy rate
BRL appreciated to strongest since May 2024 on EM revaluation and EU-Mercosur deal

data_quality_summary.notes: Run ID 2026-W10. 2,737 factor updates across 75 countries with 0.90 average confidence. 605 trend estimates across 55 countries. 53 active alerts: 13 critical, 21 warning, 19 watch. Key data quality flags: Strait of Hormuz -70% traffic confirmed from multiple sources; Iran data reliability degraded due to active conflict conditions.
